channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Shooting wounds 2 at Phoenix airport, 1 other stabbed, police say
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — A shooting at Sky Harbor Airport in Phoenix left two people injured by gunfire and another person stabbed in an incident related to a family dispute, police say. Phoenix police said the shooting happened at about 9:45 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Powerful thunderstorms rumble across Texas, delaying holiday travel
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Some flights were delayed or canceled in Texas on Thursday after a line of thunderstorms started moving across parts of the state in a system the National Weather Service predicted could bring high winds, hail and possible tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman says to-be fiancé died in Christmas house fire searching for ring
item.description: A California man died in a Christmas morning house fire while searching for an engagement ring for his girlfriend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio corrections officer killed by inmate on Christmas morning
item.description: "The loss of a staff person is difficult, but to lose a family member on Christmas Day at the hands of someone in our custody is a tragedy beyond comprehension.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, NewsNation

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters that it was "necessary to wait for the end of this probe" before casting any speculation.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: Burger King is giving away free chicken sandwiches and bacon cheeseburgers
item.description: Burger King is still spreading holiday cheer with after Christmas food deals. The burger chain has celebrated the holidays all month long with its 31-day deal promotion for rewards members and has saved some of its best deals for the end of the year. On Friday, Dec. 27, and Monday, Dec.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Year-end travel: What you need to know about post-holiday trips
item.description: CHICAGO (NewsNation) — Millions of Americans are traveling on Thursday as a record-breaking post-holiday rush continues. The Transportation Security Administration anticipates Friday to be one of the season's busiest days, with 40 million passengers projected to be screened on New Year's Day.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida woman arrested after allegedly breaking in, stabbing person over pizza delivery tip
item.description: Armed with a knife, the suspect began attacking one of the victims, allegedly upset about the tip she received during an earlier delivery, deputies said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cat food distributed to California recalled after positive bird flu test; 1 cat dead
item.description: The product was sold through distributors in Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Maryland, Michigan, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island and Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why this Mexican American woman played a vital role in the US sacramental peyote trade
item.description: MIRANDO CITY, Texas (AP) — The late Amada Cardenas was called many things — the “angel of peyote,” the “peyote rose” or simply “Grandma Amada.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH and JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Peyote sacred to Native Americans threatened by psychedelic renaissance and development
item.description: Peyote, a cactus that contains mescaline, a hallucinogen, grows naturally in South Texas and northern Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Bharath And Jessie Wardarski, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Republicans plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress to push through long-sought changes that include voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is known about the Kazakhstan plane crash that killed 38 of the 67 on board
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner has crashed in Kazakhstan after being diverted, killing 38 of 67 people on board.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Defiant victims who turned the tables on attackers in 2024
item.description: Crime victims across the country this year took it upon themselves to fight back against their attackers, with video capturing how the incidents went down.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Retired Georgia news anchor falls into ravine, rescued by helicopter in elaborate rescue: report
item.description: Carol Sbarge, a longtime reporter and anchor, fell into a ravine while on a hike at the Chattahoochee National Forest with her boyfriend, prompting an elaborate airlift.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces ambassador to Panama amid fight over canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced Miami-Dade County Commissioner Kevin Marino Cabrera as his pick for U.S. ambassador to Panama. The announcement comes as Trump is sparking a feud with the U.S. ally over control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Beyoncé's performance highlighted Netflix's NFL debut on Christmas Day
item.description: Beyoncé provided more excitement than either game during Netflix’s NFL debut on Christmas Day. Riding into her halftime appearance on a white horse, the 32-time Grammy winner rocked her hometown Houston crowd with a nearly 13-minute performance on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Undersea power cable linking Finland and Estonia hit by outage, prompting investigation
item.description: FRANKFURT, Germany (AP) — A power cable linking Finland and Estonia under the Baltic Sea suffered an outage, prompting an investigation, Finnish Prime Minister Petteri Orpo said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New York City taxi cab plows into group of pedestrians on Christmas Day, leaves at least 6 injured
item.description: At least six people are injured after a taxi cab slammed into a group of individuals at a busy intersection in New York City on Christmas Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump campaign won't pay $49,000 in bills for Grand Rapids rallies
item.description: That is forcing the city to write off those expenses as uncollectable and to reevaluate policies for future campaign visits.
item.dc:creator: Ken Kolker, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois dentist gets 15 years for stealing patients' drugs, doing surgeries without proper pain management
item.description: Phillip M. Jensen, 64, was sentenced on Dec. 18, specifically for stealing fentanyl from his patients, injecting them with adulterated drugs and performing surgery without proper pain management.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Holesha, NewsNation

item.title: Stunning photos show lava erupting from Hawaii's Kilauea volcano
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the world's most active volcanoes spewed lava into the air for a second straight day on Tuesday. The eruption of Kilauea volcano on Hawaii's Big Island has stayed within the mountain's summit caldera inside Hawaii Volcanoes National Park. No homes were threatened.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man arraigned on murder charges in NYC subway death fanned flames with a shirt, prosecutors say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The man accused of burning a woman to death inside a New York City subway train used a shirt to fan the flames, a prosecutor said Tuesday at his arraignment on murder charges. Sebastian Zapeta, 33, who federal immigration officials said is a Guatemalan citizen who entered the U.S.
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden signs a bill officially making the bald eagle the national bird of the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The bald eagle, a symbol of the power and strength of the United States for more than 240 years, earned an overdue honor on Tuesday: It officially became the country's national bird.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden takes heat for vetoing bill that would add new judgeships
item.description: Federal judiciary leadership and some lawmakers are criticizing President Biden for vetoing a bill that would’ve added 66 new federal judgeships amid growing caseloads.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Eggs added to FDA's updated healthy claim food list
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has added eggs to its updated list of healthy foods along with fish and vegetables
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Mega Millions: Jackpot now over $1 billion
item.description: The Mega Millions jackpot has only been won three times in 2024, the fewest wins in a single year since the game began.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 people rescued when California pier partially collapses
item.description: A portion of the Santa Cruz Wharf has collapsed and is floating away Monday, according to the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, ABC 4

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KRON4

item.title: Walmart illegally opened bank accounts for 1 million drivers, lawsuit alleges
item.description: Walmart and fintech company Branch Messenger are being sued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for allegedly forcing more than one million delivery workers to use expensive deposit accounts to access their paychecks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: American Airlines technical issue, bad weather leads to flight delays
item.description: About 1,000 American Airlines flights were delayed as of Tuesday afternoon, according to FlightAware.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: Packers clinch playoff berth with 1st shutout in NFL this season, 34-0 over Saints
item.description: With a dominant defense leading the way, the Green Bay Packers clinched a playoff berth by producing their most lopsided win in a decade.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bill Clinton is hospitalized with a fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to MedStar Georgetown University Hospital in Washington after developing a fever. The 78-year-old was hospitalized in the “afternoon for testing and observation," Angel Urena, Clinton's deputy chief of staff, said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyer blasts New York officials for 'staged' perp walk
item.description: The attorney for Luigi Mangione, who has been accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, slammed New York City officials for what she referred to as a “staged” perp walk for her client last week.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Van bursts into flames inside California tunnel
item.description: Emergency crews responded to a vehicle fire inside the Caldecott Tunnel in the San Francisco Bay Area on Monday morning, according to the California Highway Patrol. Eastbound Highway 24 is shut down inside Bore 1 after a minivan caught fire at around 9:54 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, WFLA, NBC
